c_lines: Words: Frederick W. Faber, 1849. Refrain by James G. Walton, 1874.
Music: 'St. Catherine' Henri F. Hemy (1818-1888).  Setting: James G. Walton, 1874.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2006 Revision.

1.raw_text: Faith of our fathers, living still, In spite of dungeon, fire and sword; O how our hearts beat high with joy Whene'er we hear that glorious Word! Faith of our fathers, holy faith! We will be true to thee till death.

2.raw_text: Faith of our fathers, we will strive To win all nations unto Thee; And through the truth that comes from God, We all shall then be truly free. fathers, holy faith! We will be true to thee till death.

3.raw_text: Faith of our fathers, we will love Both friend and foe in all our strife; And preach Thee, too, as love knows how By kindly words and virtuous life. fathers, holy faith! We will be true to thee till death.
